$comment: c12 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-FNPTR-BRACE-INIT: brace-init of a function-pointer struct member (the SQLite vtable idiom). ROOT was a function-to-pointer DECAY gap — the field is Ptr<FnSig> but the bare function name stayed an undecayed FnSig designator. FIX: CST->HIR `coerce` gained an `FnSig -> Ptr<FnSig>` decay arm (synthetic Cast -> MIR Bitcast over the function GlobalAddr), gated on the same-signature predicate isAssignable uses (a signature mismatch stays a loud verifier failure). RED-ON-DISABLE: remove the arm and `struct Ops a = { dbl };` fails (H_VerifierFailure: ConstructAggregate child type mismatch). NOTE: a file-scope/global vtable with a relocation-bearing member is a SEPARATE pre-existing limitation (global aggregate initializer with symbol-address members, the aggregate analog of F5) — out of scope here. The pe arm RUNS on the windows leg; elf/macho/arm64 on the linux/darwin/qemu legs; the release arm witnesses the optimizer over the indirect call.
